vitals.date_specific: 2026: December 18-19 (KOM Friday, main Gran Fondo Saturday Dec 19)
vitals.field_size: Approximately 500 historically (2022, 2025 editions); no official 2026 cap published
vitals.feed_zones: Neot Smadar, Camp Shagor (Route 12), and the finish; neutral support only — team chase cars are prohibited

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61.

course_description.character: A genuine Arava/Negev desert route, but mixed rather than continuously mountainous. The 140 km course opens with about 52 fast, flat kilometers on the Arava Road (Route 90) before the principal climb — Mt. Ayit/Ketura toward Neot Smadar, roughly 4.2 km at 6.5% average with pitches to 14%. From there, riders face about 60 km of exposed, rolling hills on Route 12, locally nicknamed the 'bathtubs' for their repeated short climbs and descents, before a finish around Netafim Pass/Mount Yoash and a neutralized descent back toward Eilat (only 116.8 km/1,350 m of the full 140 km is competitively timed). Feed stations sit at Neot Smadar, Camp Shagor on Route 12, and the finish; team cars are prohibited and neutral support runs front and rear instead.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Route 12 'bathtubs' — roughly 60 km of exposed, repeatedly rolling desert hills after the Mt. Ayit/Ketura climb, where accumulated fatigue and Arava wind do more damage than any single gradient.
suffering_zones: Arava Road (Route 90) opening — 52 km of flat, fast, fully exposed desert highway with little shelter from wind
Mt. Ayit/Ketura climb toward Neot Smadar — 4.2 km at 6.5% average, ramps to 14%, the course's one sustained climb
Route 12 'bathtubs' — roughly 60 km of short, repeated rolling hills that wear riders down more through volume than steepness
Netafim Pass/Mount Yoash finish — the last climbing before a neutralized, non-competitive descent back to Eilat

history.origin_story: First run in 2021, with a second edition in 2022 before a pause; Shvoong Sport Events took over production and revived it in 2025, working with the Israel Cycling Federation, Eilat Municipality and Astral Hotels. A planned 2023 UCI Gran Fondo World Series debut was cancelled due to the war.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Real Desert Fondo, Smaller Than Advertised
biased_opinion.summary: Gran Fondo Eilat is a legitimate Israeli road event through genuinely dramatic Arava/Negev desert terrain — closed roads, electronic timing, prize money, federation involvement and leading Israeli racers all real. But the database's prior figures overstated it on two fronts: the course is 140 km/1,393 m, not 136.8 km/1,400 m, and the field has run around 500 in the 2022 and 2025 editions (the 2021 launch was smaller, roughly 200), not the prior '1000+' figure, which had no supporting source. The event has also had a broken run: launched 2021, a second edition in 2022, then a gap, a cancelled 2023 UCI Gran Fondo World Series debut due to the war, and a 2025 revival under new organizer Shvoong Sport Events. It is not currently on the UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendar despite past ambitions to join it.
strengths: Genuinely dramatic Arava/Negev desert terrain — a real climb (Mt. Ayit/Ketura, 4.2 km at 6.5%, to 14%) plus long exposed rolling sections, not just flat highway
Closed roads, electronic timing, prize money and Israel Cycling Federation involvement — a serious production, not a casual ride
A rare December desert fondo — a genuine off-season option for riders escaping northern-hemisphere winter
Revived in 2025 after a rocky 2022-2024 stretch, showing organizer commitment to keeping the event alive
weaknesses: Field size ran around 500 in the 2022 and 2025 editions (the 2021 launch was smaller, roughly 200), not the 1000+ this database previously claimed — no source supports four figures
Interrupted history: launched 2021, ran 2022, a planned 2023 UCI Gran Fondo World Series debut was cancelled by the war, then a gap until the 2025 revival
Not currently a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, despite past ambitions to join the series
Only three completed editions (2021, 2022, 2025) — a short, interrupted track record versus established desert fondos elsewhere
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A real, well-run desert road fondo with genuine Negev terrain and serious federation backing — just go in knowing it's a roughly 500-rider regional event with an interrupted history, not the four-figure international fixture the prior listing implied.

final_verdict.one_liner: A real Negev desert climb and long rolling 'bathtubs' — a serious ~500-rider Israeli fondo with an interrupted history, not a four-figure international one.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a genuine December desert fondo with a real climb and closed-road organization, and don't mind a smaller field and a race that's had gaps in its calendar. Confirm the current year's dates directly with the organizer given the event's history of schedule disruption.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a similarly rare winter/off-season desert road event, look for other Israeli or regional Middle Eastern fondos on the Israel Cycling Federation calendar.

videos.title: הסרטון הרישמי! Gran Fondo Eilat 2025
videos.description:
צפו עכשיו בסרטון הרישמי מגראן פונדו אילת 2025 

צלמים:  יניב כהן, אונאל גמליאל
רחפן: יניב כהן

================

קוגוט ובצלאל ניצחו בגראן פונדו אילת 2025 , סילברברג הדהים ב-GC!

הספרינטר הבכיר היה חלק מבריחה איכותית וניצח בספרינט הסיום לפני עמית קינן וסער עידן. נועם סילברברג הפתיע וזכה בדירוג הכללי. אצל הנשים, בצלאל השיגה ניצחון בקטע וב-GC לפני יעל דגן ואנטונינה רזניקוב


אתר התחרות: https://gran-fondo.shvoong.co.il
כתבת סיכום: https://tinyurl.com/rsw2fr85

eligibility.notes: CONFIRMED via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 8. Resolved the Dec 11 vs Dec 18-19 date conflict: the competitive weekend is December 18-19, 2026 (15 km KOM Friday, main Gran Fondo Saturday Dec 19); the Dec 11-12 listing was an unconfirmed third-party page recycling 2023 copy. Corrected this wave: distance_km 136.8->140.0, elevation_m 1400->1393 (organizer's current route page); field_size '1000+'->'around 500 in the 2022/2025 editions' (no source supports four figures; a follow-up sol review found the 2021 launch was smaller, roughly 200, so 'roughly 500 historically' overstated the inaugural edition); feed_zones 'chase cars for provisioning'->neutral support at Neot Smadar/Camp Shagor/finish (team chase cars are explicitly prohibited by the organizer). founded corrected to 2021. History corrected: launched 2021, ran 2022, a planned 2023 UCI Gran Fondo World Series debut was cancelled due to the war, then a gap until Shvoong Sport Events revived it in 2025. Not currently UCI Gran Fondo World Series affiliated.
